Package org.solenopsis.soap.tooling

Class MLDataDefinition

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.tooling.Metadata
org.solenopsis.soap.tooling.MLDataDefinition
public class MLDataDefinition extends Metadata

Java class for MLDataDefinition complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="MLDataDefinition">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}Metadata">
       <sequence>
         <element name="developerName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="entityDeveloperName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="excludedFields" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="includedFields" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="joinFields" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}MLField" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="parentDefinitionDevName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="scoringFilter" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}MLFilter" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="segmentFilter" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}MLFilter" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="trainingFilter" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}MLFilter" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="type" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}MLDataDefinitionType"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

      value - allowed object is String

    • getExcludedFields

      public List<String> getExcludedFields()
      Gets the value of the excludedFields property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the excludedFields property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getExcludedFields().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String

      Returns:
      The value of the excludedFields property.

    • getIncludedFields

      public List<String> getIncludedFields()
      Gets the value of the includedFields property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the includedFields property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getIncludedFields().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String

      Returns:
      The value of the includedFields property.

    • getJoinFields

      public List<MLField> getJoinFields()
      Gets the value of the joinFields property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the joinFields property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getJoinFields().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list MLField

      Returns:
      The value of the joinFields property.
